arabic: رَبِّ اشْرَحْ لِي صَدْرِي وَيَسِّرْ لِي أَمْرِي
transliteration: Rabbish-rah li sadri wa yassir li amri
translation: My Lord, expand for me my chest and ease for me my task.

insights.meaning: The dua of Prophet Musa (AS) before his great mission — asking for inner capacity and outward ease.
insights.benefits: Especially powerful before important tasks, difficult conversations, or moments of anxiety.
insights.when: Before any important undertaking, before studying, before speaking publicly.
insights.explanation: Musa (AS) made this dua before facing Pharaoh. It teaches us to ask Allah for internal strength (expanded chest) before external ease.

translation: Allah is sufficient for us, and He is the best disposer of affairs.

insights.meaning: The statement of complete trust — declaring that Allah alone is enough and that His management of affairs is perfect.
insights.benefits: Ibrahim (AS) said this when thrown in the fire. The Prophet (SAW) said this in the Battle of Uhud. It is the ultimate declaration of tawakkul.
insights.when: In times of fear, uncertainty, when feeling overwhelmed, or when facing opposition.
insights.explanation: This phrase was uttered by both Prophet Ibrahim (AS) facing the fire, and by the Prophet Muhammad (SAW) and his companions when warned of an army gathering against them. In both cases, it preceded a blessing from Allah.
